Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that excludes cases where there is a specific sequence of activities with a time condition

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to find cases that have Approve Purchase Requisition followed immediately by Create Order followed immediately by Confirm Order with no other events in between Then among those cases check if the time between Create Order and Confirm Order is more than 5 days Finally exclude all events from those cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the problem says exclude them from your final result You should only return events from cases that do not meet the above criteria So the final result is all events except those in cases that have both the sequence and the timing condition

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So first I need to find all case ids that have the sequence where the three activities are consecutive and the time between Create Order and Confirm Order is greater than 5 days Then select all events from event log where case id is not in that set

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need three consecutive events in the same case with activities in order Approve Purchase Requisition then Create Order then Confirm Order So for a given case the events are ordered by timestamp We need to find three consecutive rows where activity1 is Approve Purchase Requisition activity2 is Create Order activity3 is Confirm Order

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: One approach is to use window functions to get the next event in the same case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: for a given row if activity is Approve Purchase Requisition then lead activity is Create Order and lead activity two rows ahead is Confirm Order that would mean three consecutive events with those activities

Name: Validation - PE
Text: this would identify cases where there exists at least one occurrence of the three consecutive activities in order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the time condition is between the Create Order event and the Confirm Order event of that sequence So for the sequence the Create Order is the second event Confirm Order is the third So the time between them is the third timestamp minus the second timestamp

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait perhaps better to use window functions to get the next event For each event get the next event in the same case Then look for rows where activity equals Create Order next activity equals Confirm Order and also ensure that the previous event is Approve Purchase Requisition

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: for the Create Order event the previous event should be Approve Purchase Requisition and the next event should be Confirm Order Then the time between this event timestamp and next timestamp should be greater than 5 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: this correctly identifies the cases where there is a sequence of three consecutive activities with the time condition

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the bad cases common table expression selects case ids that have at least one such sequence Then the main query selects all events not in bad cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: if a case has multiple occurrences of the sequence the distinct case id means even if a case has multiple bad sequences it is only included once which is correct because we need to exclude the entire case if it has at least one such sequence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the problem says more than 5 days so the condition is greater than interval 5 days not greater than or equal
